perturbation.original_text: Confide ntial Inf ormation means any information disclosed by one party (the ‘ Discloser ’) to the other (the ‘Recipient ’) relating directly or indirectly t o Name of Technology/Project, file # which is identified by the Discloser, either orally or in writing, as conf idential , eith er at the time of disclosure or, if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing within thirty (30) days following the original disclosure .
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Material means any information disclosed by one party (the ‘ Discloser ’) to the other (the ‘Recipient ’) relating directly or indirectly t o Name of Technology/Project, file # which is identified by the Discloser, either orally or in writing, as conf idential , eith er at the time of disclosure or, if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing within thirty (30) days following the original disclosure .
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Protected Material' in Section 1. This introduces confusion, as the rest of the document consistently uses 'Confidential Information'. This change contradicts the sections which assume that 'Confidential Information' is the governing term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may only use the Confidential Information for the purpose of type of purpose /or a research collaboration and not for any commerci al purpose (‘Permitted Purpose ’). Recipient must n ot use the Confidential Information for any other purpose without the prior written approval of Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Material' creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' refers specifically to 'Confidential Information'. If the document now uses 'Protected Material' instead, it's unclear whether the obligations in the 'Contradicted Text' still apply to the information now labeled as 'Protected Material', creating a direct conflict regarding the usage and protection of the disclosed information. The explanation clearly states that this change introduces confusion, as the rest of the document consistently uses 'Confidential Information', directly contradicting sections that assume 'Confidential Information' is the governing term.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may only use the Confidential Information for the purpose of type of purpose /or a research collaboration and not for any commerci al purpose (‘Permitted Purpose ’). Recipient must n ot use the Confidential Information for any other purpose without the prior written approval of Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may only use the Confidential Data for the purpose of type of purpose /or a research collaboration and not for any commerci al purpose (‘Permitted Purpose ’). Recipient must n ot use the Confidential Information for any other purpose without the prior written approval of Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Confidential Data' in Section 4. This creates uncertainty about whether 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' are the same, leading to potential disputes over the scope of permitted use. This is contradictory to the part of the text in the same section which assumed they're both equal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may only use the Confidential Data for the purpose of type of purpose /or a research collaboration and not for any commerci al purpose (‘Permitted Purpose ’). Recipient must n ot use the Confidential Information for any other purpose without the prior written approval of Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation clearly states that changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' creates uncertainty about whether the terms are the same. This uncertainty leads to potential disputes over the scope of permitted use. Since the changed text exists in the same section, and it assumes 'Confidential Data' and 'Confidential Information' are equal in use, the contradiction is a direct conflict, leading to an inconsistency in the document as described in the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient must keep the Confidential Information in confidence. Re cipient may only disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, directors, officers, agents, students (in University’s case) and consultants who have a need -to-know the Confidential Informatio n for the Permitted Purpose , provided that they are adv ised of the conf idential nature of the Confidential Information and are under an obligation to maintain its confidentiality . Recipient must not otherwise disclose Confidential In formation to any person or third party without the prior written approval of D iscloser.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient must keep the Confidential Material in confidence. Re cipient may only disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, directors, officers, agents, students (in University’s case) and consultants who have a need -to-know the Confidential Informatio n for the Permitted Purpose , provided that they are adv ised of the conf idential nature of the Confidential Information and are under an obligation to maintain its confidentiality . Recipient must not otherwise disclose Confidential In formation to any person or third party without the prior written approval of D iscloser.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5, 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Confidential Material' in the first sentence only. This generates confusion over what exactly needs to be kept confidential, as the subsequent sentences refer to 'Confidential Information'. This contradiction arises within the same section. The use of Material isn't defined anywhere else.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient must keep the Confidential Material in confidence. Re cipient may only disclose the Confidential Information to its employees, directors, officers, agents, students (in University’s case) and consultants who have a need -to-know the Confidential Informatio n for the Permitted Purpose , provided that they are adv ised of the conf idential nature of the Confidential Information and are under an obligation to maintain its confidentiality . Recipient must not otherwise disclose Confidential In formation to any person or third party without the prior written approval of D iscloser.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Material' in the first sentence, while the subsequent sentences and the contradicted text maintain the original term 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding which term is correct and what exactly needs to be kept confidential. The term 'Confidential Material' is not defined, leading to confusion and making the obligation unclear. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient must use at least the same standard of care in protec ting the confidentiality of the Confidential Information as it uses in protecting its own information of a sim ilar nature and, in any event, no less than a reasonabl e standard of ca re. Recipient must notify Discloser promptly upon discovery that any Confidential Information has been accessed or otherwise acquired by or disclosed to an unauthorized person.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient must use at least the same standard of care in protec ting the confidentiality of the Sensitive Data as it uses in protecting its own information of a sim ilar nature and, in any event, no less than a reasonabl e standard of ca re. Recipient must notify Discloser promptly upon discovery that any Confidential Information has been accessed or otherwise acquired by or disclosed to an unauthorized person.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced by 'Sensitive Data' in the first sentence of Section 6. This change is problematic because 'Sensitive Data' is not defined anywhere in the agreement. This leads to uncertainty regarding the standard of care, creating a contradiction in the same section. It's not clear if Sensitive Data is the same as confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient must use at least the same standard of care in protec ting the confidentiality of the Sensitive Data as it uses in protecting its own information of a sim ilar nature and, in any event, no less than a reasonabl e standard of ca re. Recipient must notify Discloser promptly upon discovery that any Confidential Information has been accessed or otherwise acquired by or disclosed to an unauthorized person.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'Confidential Information', which is understood within the document. The changed text replaces this with 'Sensitive Data' in the first sentence, which is undefined, leading to ambiguity. The second sentence still refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether 'Sensitive Data' and 'Confidential Information' are the same. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', because the contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, namely regarding the standard of care. As the changed text is in the same location as the contradicted text, it causes an in-text contradiction in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Private Data and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7, 'Confidential Information' is replaced by 'Private Data' in the first sentence only. The term 'Private Data' is never defined within the contract, which creates a high degree of ambiguity. Does this only apply to a subset of the Confidential Information? This is a contradiction inside of the section itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If requested in writing by Discloser , Recipient must cease using, return to Discloser and/or destroy all Private Data and any copies of Confidential Information in its possessi on or control. Recipient may retain one archival copy of such Confidential Information for the sole purpose of establishing the extent of the disclosure of such Confidential Information, p rovided that such information is not used by Recipient for any other purpose and is subject to the confident iality requirements set out in this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Data' in the first sentence of the section. 'Private Data' is not defined in the contract, which introduces ambiguity. The remaining part of the section still uses 'Confidential Information,' creating a contradiction within the section itself, as the changed sentence may apply to a different or undefined subset of the information covered by the agreement. The contradiction text is the changed text. This ambiguity satisfies the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction", because the contract now "uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations."

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement. This Agreement does not obligate the Discloser to make any d isclosure of Con fidential Information to the Recipient or require the parties to enter into any business relationship or further agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Company Secrets remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement. This Agreement does not obligate the Discloser to make any d isclosure of Con fidential Information to the Recipient or require the parties to enter into any business relationship or further agreement.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 8, 'Confidential Information' is replaced by 'Company Secrets' in the first sentence. This creates confusion as to whether 'Company Secrets' falls under the original definition of 'Confidential Information', and it is undefined in this contract. This is a contradiction inside of the section itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Company Secrets remains the property of Discloser and no licence or any other rights to the Confidential Information is granted to Recipient under this Agreement. This Agreement does not obligate the Discloser to make any d isclosure of Con fidential Information to the Recipient or require the parties to enter into any business relationship or further agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Secrets' in a sentence, but only 'Confidential Information' is defined in the contract. Since the contradicted text remains the same as the changed text, this means that the sentence now refers to an undefined term, 'Company Secrets', while still referencing 'Confidential Information'. This introduces ambiguity and creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Company Secrets' falls under the definition of 'Confidential Information', and the term is undefined.
